name: Annah

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Annah guide.
sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Annah Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

notes: Annah does not have a published build section on Tychara yet, so this page uses her Tychara stat line and kit details as a fallback build profile.
Her self-healing special and Max HP scaling point cleanly toward a speed-first HP tank shell.
ATK Down on the ultimate makes faster cycling more valuable than forcing extra damage stats.

Journey Hard routing in the sheet points toward DPS - AX5.

DEF Up on the ultimate and ATK Down on the special make her a straightforward frontline lane anchor.

name: Charlotte

sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Charlotte Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

recommendedSets.label: Budget
recommendedSets.notes: The source build leans on Effect Hit support value while keeping Charlotte fast.

recommendedSets.label: Endgame
recommendedSets.notes: Swap into a harder damage shell once crit and gear quality are stable.

notes: Charlotte sits in the hybrid support-DPS lane, so speed and utility substats still matter even when you upgrade into higher damage sets.
The sheet keeps Charlotte on SPD neck and ATK% ring across both budget and late-game routes.

Action Gauge gain on the special keeps SPD valuable even on a pure single-target setup.

recommendedSets.label: Endgame

She rewards players who build for speed and control rather than only raw damage.

name: Marcille

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Marcille guide.
sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Marcille Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

notes: Marcille does not have a published build section on Tychara yet, so this page uses her Tychara stat line and kit details as a fallback build profile.
Burn and Recovery Down are both attached to her damage buttons, so she benefits from a mixed crit-plus-hit stat line.
Her high base crit rate lets her reach a stable damage shell earlier than most SR casters.

name: Muriel

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Muriel guide.
sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Muriel Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

notes: Muriel’s source build is another clear example of the sheet favoring Hit support on units whose identity is tied to debuff value.
Keep her fast enough to extend and recycle debuffs instead of chasing a pure glass-cannon setup.

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Naru guide.
sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Naru Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

notes: Naru does not have a published build section on Tychara yet, so this page uses her Tychara stat line and kit details as a fallback build profile.
Her ultimate adds both SPD Down and Effect RES Down, so Hit-focused sets give her the clearest team value.
She still carries attack-scaling single-target skills, so ATK% and crit remain worthwhile once speed and hit are covered.

name: Rosaria

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Rosaria guide.
sourceContext.citationText: Source page: Rosaria Guide - Star Savior | Kamigame.

recommendedSets.label: Burst swap
recommendedSets.notes: Use this once Crit Rate is already stable from substats, arcana, or other account pieces.

notes: Rosaria is a fast Chaos Ranger burst DPS who turns crits on her ultimate into Inferno stacks, then cashes those stacks into a lethal special.
Her ultimate applies teamwide Defense Down, gives Rosaria +50% action gauge, and is the centerpiece of both her PvE burst shell and her PvP rush opener.
Build Crit Rate first. Once that is secure, Destruction-style burst setups scale harder than the safer Insight baseline.
She works especially well with turn-gauge supports because her own opener already pushes her forward into a second action.

sourceContext.lead: Translated and adapted from Kamigame's Scarlet guide.

notes: Scarlet does not have a published build section on Tychara yet, so this page uses her Tychara stat line and kit details as a fallback build profile.

Her AoE ultimate carries SPD Down, so Hit remains a high-value secondary stat instead of a throwaway roll.
